CheckTitle: Permanent tablespaces are encrypted with TDE
Description: Checks that each permanent application tablespace is encrypted with **Transparent Data Encryption (TDE)**. SYSTEM and SYSAUX are excluded and reported separately by DBSAT (finding ENCRYPT.TDE).
Risk: Unencrypted tablespaces expose their data to anyone who can read the underlying files: OS administrators, stolen backups or disks, and direct data file parsing all bypass database authentication entirely.

Code.CLI: CREATE TABLESPACE <name> DATAFILE ... ENCRYPTION USING 'AES256' DEFAULT STORAGE (ENCRYPT);
Code.Other:
1. Configure and open the TDE keystore
2. Encrypt existing tablespaces online: ALTER TABLESPACE <name> ENCRYPTION ONLINE USING 'AES256' ENCRYPT
3. Create new tablespaces encrypted by default (ENCRYPT_NEW_TABLESPACES=ALWAYS)

Recommendation.Text: Encrypt all permanent tablespaces with TDE, use online conversion for existing ones and set ENCRYPT_NEW_TABLESPACES so future tablespaces are encrypted automatically.
